name: context-menu
description: A right-click context menu opened at the pointer. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012, Wave 2): open-state, pointer position, items and selection live in typed Java; the server re-renders, the client morphs. A tiny CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer captures the contextmenu gesture (no Lit, no @floating-ui/dom). Replaces the <lv-context-menu> Lit island. WAI-ARIA APG menu pattern (role=menu of role=menuitem).
docs: Register ContextMenuComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call) and supply the real `items` list (override the default in your copy). Render it via the wire mount / `@template.lievit.context-menu(...)`; put your right-clickable content in the owned `data-context-menu-trigger` region (NOT a slot). Call `enhanceContextMenus(runtime)` once from main.ts after `startLievit()` so a right-click opens the menu at the pointer. A selection arms `selectedKey` via $set; read it (or add server authz on a `closeAfterSelect`) in your copy. The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/context-menu")` name + the io.lievit.wire package are yours to change once copied.
